titleStmt.title: SII 16.105: original edition by H. K. Narasimhaswami and G. S. Gai
No. 105. (A.R. No. 479 of 1906.) GŌPAVARAM, PRODDATUR TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.

summary.p: On two faces of a slab set up in the middle of the village.
This is dated in Śaka 1455, Vijaya, Jyēshṭha śu. 12, Sunday, corresponding to 1533 A.D., June 4. But the week day would be wednesday and not Sunday.

It records a gift of land to God Hanumanta consecrated by Vāgumaḍugu Vīramareḍḍi in Gōpavaram included in Rāmēśvaram-Podaṭūru in Ghaṇḍikōṭa-sīma by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Jagatāpi Kañchchirāju Raṅgaya-Dēvachōḍamahārāju. Gōpavaram is said to have been obtained by the donor as uṁbaḷi from Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Salakarāju Tirumalayyadēva-mahārāju.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī jayādbhudaya śālivā
hana śakavaruṣaṁbulu 1455 A
gunēṭi vijeya saṁvatsara jēṣṭa śu 12 Ā
śrīma(ṁ)Nmahāmaṁḍalēśvara jagatāpi kaṁccirā
ju raṁgayadēvacōḍamahārājulu rāmēśvara
podaṭūrilō gōpavaraṁpalenu vāgumaḍugu
vīramareḍinūtanapratiṣṭagā nilipina hanumaṁta
rāvuniki naivēdyānakugānu yiccina bhūdāna dha
rmaśāsanaṁ . svasti . Ōnama . śrīma(n)nmahārājarā
jādhirāja rāja paramēśvara śrīvīrapratāpa śrīAcyu
tadēvarāyamahārāyalu vijayanagaraṁmaṁ
du vajrasiṁhāsanāsīnuṁḍai pṛthvīsāmrājyaṁ nēI
cunnuṁḍa śrīmaNmahāmaṁḍalīśvara sakalarāju
pa tirumalayadēva mahārājulu māku pāliṁcina
gaṁḍikōṭasīmalōni rāmēśvaraṁ prodaṭūru mā
ku vuṁbaḷikiccina Aṁdulōni gōpavarānanu
hanumaṁtuniki pūrvana naḍacē cēnu kha 0 .
yiṁdumunnu pulikavaraṁ lakanacēnistaḷaṁ nē
mu dhāravōsi yiccina cēnu kha 0 4 nalu
nunu sahiraṇyōdaka dā
yi dharmānaku yava
ru tappinanu gaṁgakarta gōbrā(ṁ)hmaṇula
vadhiṁcina pāpāna pōvuvāru . svada
ttā dviguṇaṁ puṇyaṁ paradattānupāla
naṁ . paradattāpahārēṇa svadattaṁ ni
ṣphalaṁ bhavēt . śrīśrīśrī
śrīrāma . On one side of this broken slab is the following:
kāśegaṁga
kuṁḍu ko
sa¨rvvayya
sēsina hanu-
maṁtuḍu
śrīśrīśrī .
Digital edition of SII 16.105 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
